transcript: be equivalent to temperature. Cold air is heavier, or high pressure, and hot air is lighter, or low pressure. A classroom demonstration of this can be done with an empty aquarium, a lamp for heat, some ice cubes, and a smoke source. Heat up one end of the aquarium with the light source and cool the other end with the ice cubes. This creates areas of high and low pressure. Place something on the aquarium that shields it from outside air. Then introduce some smoke so we can see the air moving. Kids, don't do this.
